raw.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.6.
raw.plan: This pass trims expensive, clunky effects to lower the deck’s average mana value and make early turns smoother for [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]]. The additions are cheap interaction and card selection that help the deck deploy spells sooner and more consistently. The cuts remove top-heavy cards that were slowing the curve without improving the mana base.
raw.why: This pass trims expensive, clunky effects to lower the deck’s average mana value and make early turns smoother for [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]]. The additions are cheap interaction and card selection that help the deck deploy spells sooner and more consistently. The cuts remove top-heavy cards that were slowing the curve without improving the mana base.

added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] adds efficient two-mana creature removal to keep interaction cheap.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] is low-cost instant-speed removal that fits the tighter curve.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] gives inexpensive card smoothing to improve early consistency.
added.sleight of hand: [[Sleight of Hand]] helps find lands or action for just one mana.
added.thought scour: [[Thought Scour]] is a very cheap cantrip that keeps spells flowing.
added.wash away: [[Wash Away]] provides flexible low-cost counterplay, especially against commanders.

removed.body of knowledge: [[Body of Knowledge]] is a bulky five-drop that does not support the lower curve.
removed.commandeer: [[Commandeer]] is powerful but costly and situational for a casual curve-tightening pass.
removed.nexus mentality: [[Nexus Mentality]] was cut as a slower high-cost effect to reduce top-end weight.
removed.solemn simulacrum: [[Solemn Simulacrum]] is solid value but slower than the cheaper options added here.
removed.teferi, temporal pilgrim: [[Teferi, Temporal Pilgrim]] is a higher-cost payoff that was trimmed for speed.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] is an expensive finisher that sat too high on the curve.

results.why: This pass increases cheap, reliable interaction so [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] can answer opposing threats without spending much mana. [[Pongify]], [[Rapid Hybridization]], and [[Reality Shift]] give the deck efficient creature removal at casual tables. The cuts trim lower-priority value pieces to make room for clearer answers while keeping the deck focused.

added.pongify: [[Pongify]] adds one-mana instant-speed creature removal, which directly supports the interaction upgrade.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] is another efficient instant answer that improves the deck's ability to stop key creatures.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] provides flexible exile-based interaction, giving the deck a stronger answer to problematic creatures.

removed.waterbending scroll: [[Waterbending Scroll]] was cut because it contributes less immediate interaction than the new removal spell.
removed.windfall: [[Windfall]] was removed to free space for a more direct answer rather than a value-focused effect.
removed.wizard class: [[Wizard Class]] was trimmed because it is slower setup value and not a clear interaction piece.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity by cutting fast combo pieces, lock elements, and sharper competitive tools in favor of fair interaction, smoother mana, and value plays. [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] keeps a reactive, spell-heavy game plan, but the swaps make wins less abrupt and the table experience more casual. The additions also help replace trimmed excess lands with flexible spells and accessible ramp.

raw.plan: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity by cutting fast combo pieces, lock elements, and sharper competitive tools in favor of fair interaction, smoother mana, and value plays. [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] keeps a reactive, spell-heavy game plan, but the swaps make wins less abrupt and the table experience more casual. The additions also help replace trimmed excess lands with flexible spells and accessible ramp.
raw.why: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity by cutting fast combo pieces, lock elements, and sharper competitive tools in favor of fair interaction, smoother mana, and value plays. [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] keeps a reactive, spell-heavy game plan, but the swaps make wins less abrupt and the table experience more casual. The additions also help replace trimmed excess lands with flexible spells and accessible ramp.

added.dissolve: [[Dissolve]] is a straightforward counterspell that supports a fairer control plan.
added.everflowing chalice: [[Everflowing Chalice]] adds simple scalable ramp without pushing explosive turns.
added.fading hope: [[Fading Hope]] gives cheap tempo interaction that is useful without being oppressive.
added.into the roil: [[Into the Roil]] is flexible bounce that fits a more casual interactive game.
added.memory lapse: [[Memory Lapse]] adds light stack interaction while being less final than hard counters.
added.midnight clock: [[Midnight Clock]] provides slow ramp and a value refill for longer games.
added.mulldrifter: [[Mulldrifter]] brings clean card advantage on a fair creature body.
added.pongify: [[Pongify]] is efficient removal that answers threats cleanly at casual tables.
added.rapid hybridization: [[Rapid Hybridization]] adds another simple creature answer with a real drawback.
added.ravenform: [[Ravenform]] broadens removal to creatures or artifacts at a reasonable rate.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] is versatile creature interaction that keeps the deck responsive.
added.resculpt: [[Resculpt]] adds flexible removal while still leaving opponents material behind.
added.saw it coming: [[Saw It Coming]] is a solid defensive counterspell that plays well in slower games.
added.thought monitor: [[Thought Monitor]] offers card draw tied to board development instead of combo lines.
added.wash away: [[Wash Away]] gives efficient commander-focused interaction for multiplayer play.
added.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] improves early mana development in a fair, reliable way.

removed.island: A second [[Island]] was trimmed because the deck gained more ramp and lower-impact interaction.
removed.commandeer: [[Commandeer]] is a swingy high-power free spell that overshoots a casual target.
removed.harbinger of the seas: [[Harbinger of the Seas]] can create frustrating mana denial that is less casual-friendly.
removed.high tide: [[High Tide]] pushes explosive turns that the casual pass is trying to reduce.
removed.jace, wielder of mysteries: [[Jace, Wielder of Mysteries]] was removed to break up alternate-win combo pressure.
removed.laboratory maniac: [[Laboratory Maniac]] leaves alongside the dedicated self-mill combo finish.
removed.leveler: [[Leveler]] mainly supports all-in combo kills that this pass is dialing back.
removed.lightning greaves: [[Lightning Greaves]] was cut as a less essential protection piece for this softer build.
removed.muddle the mixture: [[Muddle the Mixture]] is a strong tutor-like combo enabler that raises deck sharpness.
removed.narset, parter of veils: [[Narset, Parter of Veils]] can shut down opponents’ card draw in a way that feels harsher than casual.
removed.retraction helix: [[Retraction Helix]] is a combo-oriented trick that is less needed in a casual pass.
removed.snap: [[Snap]] can enable mana-positive lines and higher-tempo turns than desired here.
removed.thought lash: [[Thought Lash]] was removed with the self-decking combo package.
removed.tidespout tyrant: [[Tidespout Tyrant]] is a powerful engine that can snowball and loop too easily for casual tables.
removed.valley floodcaller: [[Valley Floodcaller]] was cut as a narrower synergy piece in favor of broader casual utility.

Overall, the deck becomes more reliable and better at advancing its main game plan at casual power.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.53.
results.why: This pass tightens the curve by replacing slower, off-color, and land-search-heavy cards with cheap sacrifice, recursion, and death-payoff pieces that better fit [[Accursed Marauder]]. The additions make the deck more consistent at converting creatures into cards, mana, and drain while keeping mana cleaner for Commander color identity. It also adds a few efficient utility lands and graveyard tools so the deck can play a faster, more focused game.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s functional curve by replacing expensive, slower pieces with cheaper cantrips, ramp, and interaction that smooth early turns. The result is a more focused Modern plan that reaches impactful Eldrazi turns with less clunky top-end.

With no explicit additions in this change list, the main goal is a cleaner, legal configuration rather than an archetype shift.
